title: Rose Bush - Traditional Pink Old English Scented Rose Bush in a 5 Litre Pot Ready to Plant
features: Classic Old English Charm - Enjoy the timeless beauty of traditional pink blooms, shaped in the iconic Old English style. This rose bush produces full, romantic flowers that bring elegance and cottage-garden character to any outdoor space.

Strong, Sweet Fragrance - Bred for its exceptional scent, this variety fills the garden with a rich, classic rose perfume. Perfect for planting near seating areas, paths or patios where the fragrance can be fully appreciated.

Fast Establishment - Supplied as a well-rooted plant in a generous 5L pot, ready to plant straight into borders, beds or large containers. Gives you a strong start with quicker establishment and reliable first-season growth.

Long Flowering Season - Expect repeat flushes of blooms from early summer into autumn. This hardy, disease-resistant rose provides months of vibrant colour and is an excellent choice for perennial planting schemes.
description: Product Description Grow Your Own Rose Garden Put together the rose garden of your dreams with our fantastic variety of roses. Simple to care for, roses will last a long time once established in your garden, bringing joy for years to come. A selection of colours, patterns, sizes and scents adds intrigue to any outdoor space. Great for any Space Rose bushes can be planted in beds and borders in the garden, or grown in pots or containers for anyone with a smaller space. The Perfect Gift Send us a message to get a custom gift message added to any order, and give the gift of a rose garden to someone. Mix & Match Create your perfect rose garden today - Our selection is curated to supply the perfect roses, ready for planting straight into the garden. Will this rose be supplied in flower? As these are live, growing roses, your rose may not be in flower at the time of purchase. From SEP-APRIL roses are likely to be pruned back, as this helps growth in the springtime. Can I grow this rose in a pot? Yes, these can be planted into and grown in a pot or container. Is this ready to be planted now? Containerised roses can be planted at any time, as long as any hard frosts have passed. How often should I water rose bushes? Water deeply 1–2 times per week, depending on weather and soil type. How do I prune rose bushes? Prune in late winter or early spring before new growth begins and remove dead, weak, or crossing stems.
